mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals an imminent recession, causing equity markets to price in negative earnings revisions over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates a synchronized collapse in consumer durables demand, which transmits to corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, a positive month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term equity momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative or flat 5-day returns during easing periods, or the regime-conditional predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 215.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises signals a broad-based economic slowdown, which systematically reduces equity forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 110.0 and auto sales decline concurrently, manufacturing sector weakness propagates through supply chains, causing negative S&P 500 returns over one month during credit-constrained environments.
failure_condition: The combined low INDPRO and falling RSAFS condition predicts positive 20-day returns, or the signal's predictive power disappears when controlling for credit spreads.
